title: I Brought 6 People to the World Cup — The Only Package That Worked
excerpt: I needed to bring 6 people to 3 matches across 3 cities. Different ages, preferences, and budgets. Here's the package that saved my sanity.
content:
Planning a World Cup trip for 6 people is a nightmare.

Different flight preferences. Different food needs. Different match interests. And everyone has an opinion.

I almost gave up and went solo.

Then I discovered the group suite package, and everything changed.

## The Problem

My group:
- Me (organizer, stressed)
- My wife (wants comfort, hates crowds)
- My two teenage sons (want to be close to the action)
- My brother (budget-conscious)
- His wife (foodie, wants good dining)

Six completely different expectations. One itinerary. No way to please everyone.

## The Contender: Suite Package (4-8 guests)

This is the package that solved every problem.

**What it is:** A private suite with 4-8 seats. One price for the whole suite. You split it however you want.

**Per-person cost (6 people):** $5,500-7,000 depending on the match

## How It Solved Everything

**The seats:** We all sat together in a private box. The boys had the front row (closest to the field). My wife had a cushioned seat away from the crowd noise. Everyone happy.

**The food:** Pre-ordered from a custom menu. My brother got burgers. His wife got the tasting menu. My wife ordered gluten-free without anyone else knowing. The suite butler managed it all.

**The timing:** Each person arrived when they wanted. The concierge waited at the suite. No herding cats through security.

**The payment:** I paid for the suite. Everyone reimbursed me. Per-person it was cheaper than buying individual Platinum tickets.

## The Cost Comparison

For 6 people, 3 matches:

**Individual Platinum tickets:** $10K-18K each × 6 = $60K-108K per match
**Suite (4-8 ppl):** $22K-50K per match for the whole group

With 6 people, the suite was 40-55% cheaper per person.

And we had a private bathroom. And a butler. And didn't have to fight for space at halftime.

## The Family Advantage

If you're traveling with kids or older parents, the suite is the obvious choice.

- Private bathroom (no porta-potty horror stories)
- Climate-controlled (no freezing in open concourses)
- Space to spread out (not cramped in a single seat)
- Butler handles everything (you watch the game)

My youngest fell asleep during the second half of one match. The butler brought a blanket and a pillow without being asked. That alone was worth the price.

## What About Different Matches?

We saw 3 matches across 3 cities. The concierge booked a suite in each stadium. Same package, different venue.

They coordinated the ground transport too. One van for all 6 of us to each stadium. We never split up.

## The Verdict

If you're bringing 3 or more people to FIFA 2026, the suite package is the only way to do it without losing your mind.

Per-person cost beats individual tickets. The experience is better. And you actually get to watch the match instead of managing logistics.

[Check group suite availability for your matches] — 4-8 person packages.

*My brother said it was the best trip we've ever taken as a family. I didn't even have to argue with him about the cost.*
